z'Define SearchEngine for search dialogs.

messageboxc
Return the singleton SearchEngine instance for the process.
    The single SearchEngine saves settings between dialog instances.
    If there is not a SearchEngine already, make one.

z<Handles searching a text widget for Find, Replace, and Grep.c
Initialize Variables that save search state.
        The dialogs bind these to the UI elements present in the dialogs.

z&Return compiled cooked search pattern.z

report_errorr%

SearchEngine.getprogNc
Error: z
Pattern: z	
Offset: z
Regular expression error)
master)
strr
Z	showerrorr	
colr
SearchEngine.report_errorr
Return (lineno, matchobj) or None for forward/backward search.
        This function calls the right function with the right arguments.
        It directly return the result of that call.
        Text is a text widget. Prog is a precompiled pattern.
        The ok parameter is a bit complicated as it has two effects.
        If there is a selection, the search begin at either end,
        depending on the direction setting and ok, with ok meaning that
        the search starts with the selection. Otherwise, search begins
        at the insert mark.
        To aid progress, the search functions do not return an empty
        match at the starting position unless ok is True.
        N)
get_selectionr!
get_line_col
search_backward
search_forward)
textr-
wrap
first
last
start
liner1
resr
search_textf
SearchEngine.search_textc
}	|	r
}	|	s |
}	q d
%d.0
1.0z
2.0)
search
end)
wrapped
	startline
chars
SearchEngine.search_forwardc
end-1c
search_reverser;
index
split
len)
SearchEngine.search_backward)
__name__
__module__
__qualname__
__doc__r
sXqf|
Search backwards and return an re match object or None.
    This is done by searching forwards until there is no match.
    Prog: compiled re object with a search method returning a match.
    Chars: line of text, without \n.
    Col: stop index for the search; the limit for match.end().
    Nr@
span)
found
zFReturn tuple of 'line.col' indexes from selection or insert mark.
    z	sel.firstz
sel.lastN
insert)
z8Return (line, col) tuple of ints from 'line.col' string.rH
